blocks.body: Adjectives and Adverbs covers comparison, adjective/adverb choice, and the core contrast patterns learners confuse most.

blocks.heading: Adjectives
blocks.body:
• Adjectives describe nouns.
  • Here you work with forms such as fast, faster, the fastest.
  • This is the base for comparison.

blocks.heading: Adverbs
blocks.body:
• Adverbs appear in Adjectives vs Adverbs.
  • The focus is what the form describes and where it goes.
  • Typical pairs: careful / carefully, good / well.

blocks.heading: Degrees of Comparison
blocks.body:
• Degrees of Comparison gives you comparative and superlative forms.
  • Comparative vs Superlative helps you choose the form.
  • As ... As / Not As ... As adds equality patterns.

blocks.heading: Topics in This Section
blocks.body:
TopicLevelDescription
Degrees of ComparisonA2–B1Comparative and superlative
Comparative vs SuperlativeA2–B1Choosing the form
Equality ComparisonA2–B1as ... as / not as ... as
Adjectives vs AdverbsA2–B1Adjective or adverb

blocks.heading: Useful Pointers
blocks.body:
• Degrees of Comparison — comparative and superlative.
  • Comparative vs Superlative — choosing the form.
  • Equality Comparison — as ... as / not as ... as.
  • Adjectives vs Adverbs — good / well.

blocks.heading: Study Tips
blocks.body:
🧠

Start with Degrees of Comparison, then move to Comparative vs Superlative and Equality Comparison.

Adjectives vs Adverbs helps with good / well and -ly forms.
